items.item: Turn scattered alpha telemetry into a single playtest pulse that says whether VaultFront is fun, legible, and tournament-ready today.
items.recipe: Add server-side playtest pulse recording and summary endpoints that aggregate tutorial completion, post-match feedback, tournament actions, and retention signals into one launch-facing payload.

items.item: Make first-session onboarding measurable without adding friction.
items.recipe: Have VaultFrontTutorial post lightweight events for shown, step advance, skip, and complete so playtest pulse can distinguish confusion from churn.

items.item: Give every internal tournament a generated operator brief: state, missing slots, next actionable match, and overlay URL.
items.recipe: Extend TournamentStore bracket views with a compact operations summary and expose it through existing bracket responses so the modal can show what to run next.

items.item: Close the startup brief's stale revenue/compliance warning loop with live launch evidence instead of another static note.
items.recipe: Include playtest pulse and evidence freshness in /api/vaultfront/readiness and PROJECT_STATUS so promotion checks can see whether the alpha has current behavioral signal.

skipped.reason: VaultFront already has narrator, recap, coach, fortune, and story systems; current leverage is measuring whether those systems improve retention.
Revenue signal remains unverified and the project is internal alpha; pushing monetization before playtest pulse would violate cost discipline.

executionLog.summary: Added the in-memory VaultFront playtest pulse model plus POST and summary endpoints for tutorial, match, tournament, and retention signals.
VaultFrontTutorial now records shown, advance, skip, and complete events without blocking first-session flow.
Bracket views now include an operations brief and TournamentModal renders the next action, missing slots, next match, and overlay URL.
Readiness payloads now include the playtest pulse gate, score, freshness, and launch evidence.
